description: Write a Python fibonacci script that prints fib(10)
instructions: Create a file called /workspace/fibonacci.py that contains a function fib(n) which returns the n-th Fibonacci number (0-indexed: fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1, fib(2)=1, fib(3)=2, ...). At the end of the script, print the result of fib(10). The expected output is 55. IMPORTANT: Write the file to /workspace/fibonacci.py — that is your working directory.

files.README.md:
# Fibonacci

Write fibonacci.py that prints fib(10).
Expected output: 55

checks.name: fib(10) outputs 55
checks.command: FIB=$(find /workspace /tmp /repo -name fibonacci.py -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1) && python3 "$FIB" | grep -q 55

checks.name: fib function is correct for edge cases
checks.command: FIB=$(find /workspace /tmp /repo -name fibonacci.py -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1) && DIR=$(dirname "$FIB") && cd "$DIR" && python3 -c "from fibonacci import fib; assert fib(0) == 0; assert fib(1) == 1; assert fib(5) == 5; print('OK')"
